q_tail.question: Does the hindwing have a tail (at vein 2)?
q_tail.hint: Check the posterior margin of the hindwing for a filiform or stub-like projection at vein 2.

choices.label: Yes — hindwing is tailed
No — hindwing is tailless

q_fw_cell_silver.question: Are the cell spots on the forewing underside outlined in greenish silver?
q_fw_cell_silver.hint: Examine the underside forewing cell. Look for a metallic greenish-silver outline around the cell spots — this is distinctive and not a trick of the light.

choices.label: Yes — greenish silver outlines on FW cell spots
No — no silver outlines

q_spot_arrangement_tailed.question: How are the hindwing underside postdiscal spots in spaces 5, 6, and 7 arranged?
q_spot_arrangement_tailed.hint: Compare the alignment of the three spots. 'In echelon' means they overlap and their centres fall on a roughly straight line. 'Widely out of line' means spot 6 is displaced so its inner edge aligns with or falls inside the inner edge of spot 7 — it often overlaps the end-cell bar.

choices.label: In echelon — overlapping, centres roughly in a straight line
Widely out of line — spot 6 strongly displaced inward

q_echelon_extra_spots.question: Are there extra subcostal spots on the forewing underside?
q_echelon_extra_spots.hint: Look for a spot above the central cell spot (in space 11) or a spot at the base of space 10 on the forewing underside.

choices.label: Yes — extra spot(s) present in space 11 or at base of space 10
No — no extra subcostal spots

q_outline_extra_spots.question: Are there extra subcostal spots on the forewing underside?
q_outline_extra_spots.hint: Look for a spot above the central cell spot (in space 11) or a spot at the base of space 10 on the forewing underside.

choices.label: Yes — extra spot(s) present in space 11 or at base of space 10
No — no extra subcostal spots

q_outline_dark_area.question: Is there a distinct dark area under the forewing cell (space 1b) defined by a pale line?
q_outline_dark_area.hint: Examine the forewing underside near the cell base. The camdeo group has a characteristic darkened patch in space 1b with a clearly defined pale bordering line.

choices.label: Yes — distinct dark patch in space 1b with pale border
No — no such marking

q_upperside_green.question: Is the male upperside shining green?
q_upperside_green.hint: View the dorsal (upper) surface under natural light. Most Arhopala males are shining blue or purple. Only a few groups have shining green males. For females, answer 'No'.

choices.label: Yes — shining green upperside (male)
No — blue, purple, brown, or female

q_green_wing_shape.question: What is the forewing apex and termen shape?
q_green_wing_shape.hint: The aurea group has a more acute (pointed) forewing apex with a straighter termen. The eumolphus group has a more rounded forewing.

choices.label: More pointed apex and straighter termen
More rounded forewing

q_camdeo_tailless.question: Is there a distinct dark area under the forewing cell (space 1b) defined by a pale line?
q_camdeo_tailless.hint: Examine the forewing underside near the cell base. This pale-bordered dark patch in space 1b is the hallmark of the camdeo group.

choices.label: Yes — distinct dark patch in space 1b with pale border
No — no such marking

q_hair_tuft.question: Does the male have a recumbent hair tuft on the hindwing upperside, or an opaque/differently-textured discal patch on the forewing upperside?
q_hair_tuft.hint: These are male-only features. The hair tuft lies flat against the hindwing surface. The forewing discal patch appears opaque or differently textured compared to the surrounding shining blue.

choices.label: Yes — hair tuft or opaque discal patch visible (male)
No — no such features (or female)

q_tailless_extra_spots.question: Are there extra subcostal spots on the forewing underside?
q_tailless_extra_spots.hint: Look for a spot above the central cell spot (in space 11) or a spot at the base of space 10 on the forewing underside.

choices.label: Yes — extra spot(s) in space 11 or at base of space 10
No — no extra subcostal spots

q_spot_arrangement_tailless.question: How are the hindwing underside postdiscal spots in spaces 5, 6, and 7 arranged?
q_spot_arrangement_tailless.hint: Compare the alignment of the three spots. 'In echelon' means their centres form a roughly straight line. 'Widely out of line' means spot 6 is displaced so its inner edge aligns with or falls inside the inner edge of spot 7.

choices.label: In echelon — overlapping, centres roughly in a straight line
Widely out of line — spot 6 strongly displaced inward

g_centaurus.description: The Common Oakblue group. Diagnostic: cell spots on the forewing underside are outlined in distinctive metallic greenish silver. Typically tailed.
key_features: Forewing cell spots outlined in greenish silver (underside)
Often three metallic bands visible on hindwing underside

g_amphimuta_aedias.description: Hindwing postdiscal spots in spaces 5–7 in echelon (centres roughly in line). No extra subcostal spots on the forewing underside. No greenish-silver cell outlines. More rounded forewing (amphimuta), or slightly more acute (aedias).
key_features: Tailed or tailless depending on species

g_agesias.description: Hindwing postdiscal spots in spaces 5–7 in echelon with extra subcostal spots present on the forewing underside (in space 11 or at base of space 10).
key_features: Extra subcostal spot in FW space 11 or at base of space 10

g_cleander_agelastus.description: Hindwing postdiscal spots in spaces 5–7 widely out of line. No extra subcostal spots on the forewing underside. No distinct dark area under the forewing cell. Tailed.
key_features: HW spots 5–7 widely out of line
No dark area under FW cell (space 1b)

g_alitaeus.description: Hindwing postdiscal spots in spaces 5–7 widely out of line AND extra subcostal spots present on the forewing underside. Tailed.
key_features: HW spots 5–7 widely out of line
Extra subcostal spot in FW space 11 or at base of space 10

g_camdeo.description: Hallmark feature: a distinct dark area under the forewing cell in space 1b, defined by a pale bordering line on the forewing underside.
key_features: Distinct dark patch in FW underside space 1b with pale border

g_epimuta.description: Males have either a recumbent hair tuft on the hindwing upperside or an opaque/differently-textured discal patch on the forewing upperside. Tailless.
key_features: Male: recumbent hair tuft on HW upperside, or opaque discal patch on FW upperside

g_eumolphus.description: Males are shining green on the upperside (not blue or purple). Forewing more rounded. Tailless.

g_aurea.description: Males are shining green on the upperside. Forewing apex more acute (pointed) with a straighter termen than the eumolphus group. Tailless.
key_features: Forewing apex more pointed; termen straighter

g_anthelus_ijanensis.description: Tailless with extra subcostal spots on the forewing underside (in space 11 or at base of space 10). May include anthelus, ijanensis, and related groups — further separation requires species-level keys.
key_features: Extra subcostal spot in FW space 11 or at base of space 10

g_agelastus.description: Hindwing postdiscal spots in spaces 5–7 widely out of line. No extra subcostal spots. Tailless (in this pathway).
key_features: HW spots 5–7 widely out of line
